Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Elena's message about the expense tool readiness assessment using search_emails (query: "expense" or "relaunch" or "readiness" or "auto-categorizer"). Find Elena's April 25 email (email_elena_relaunch_deadline_01) to samira.tariq@moveops.com demanding a formal relaunch readiness assessment by Monday April 27, referencing Vectral's renewal on the line, Rachel Whitfield's April 30 board deadline, an account worth over $100K in relationship value and growing, and a go/no-go meeting scheduled for Wednesday April 29 at 10 AM with Samira, Elena, Marcus, and David.
2. Search emails for Mina's forwarded message about Vectral using search_emails (query: "Mina" or "Vectral" or "Rachel" or "renewal"). Find Mina's April 24 forward (email_mina_fwd_vectral_renewal_01) of Rachel Whitfield's email requesting three specific written confirmations from engineering: root cause identified and fixed, fix verified against Vectral's specific policy configuration, and safeguards in place to prevent recurrence. Discover the April 30 board decision deadline and that Rachel is VP of People at Vectral Systems.
3. Search Slack for engineering team updates using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "expense" or "dashboard" or "ENG-218" or "Mosaic" or "approved") or conversations_history on the #engineering channel (channel_id: C003). Find Anh's April 24 message claiming ENG-218 dashboard type validation is finished and deployed to staging, with production push planned for Monday after Lena reviews the PR. Find Dmitri's April 25 message reporting that recStip000000024 (Andre Washington, $350 coworking membership) shows Approved status dated April 22 after the auto-processing pause, suggesting the config fix was not deployed to the Mosaic environment.
4. Search Linear for open issues in the expense auto-categorizer project using linear_list_issues (project: "Expense Auto-Categorization Engine" or query: "expense" or team: "Engineering"). Find the following open tickets: ENG-214 (P1 Vectral Systems policy config, default template limits used instead of custom values, state: in_progress, assignee: Lena), ENG-215 (P1 Mosaic malformed JSON, state: in_progress, assignee: Lena), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, state: in_progress, assignee: Lena), ENG-218 (dashboard type validation, state: in_progress, assignee: Anh), ENG-216 (full audit of pilot expenses, state: in_progress, assignee: Dmitri), ENG-220 (stipend duplicate detection, state: todo, assignee: Dmitri), ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, state: todo, assignee: Samira).
5. Read comments on ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_pipeline_hardening"). Find Lena's April 24 comment stating code is complete but blocked on end-to-end testing because the staging test infrastructure (isolated policy config sandbox) that Anh was supposed to set up by Wednesday is not ready.
6. Read comments on all other expense tool tickets using linear_list_comments for ENG-214 (issueId: "issue_p1_vectral_config"), ENG-215 (issueId: "issue_p1_mosaic_malformed"), ENG-216 (issueId: "issue_audit_expenses"), ENG-218 (issueId: "issue_dashboard_type_validation"), ENG-219 (issueId: "issue_launch_readiness_checklist"), and ENG-220 (issueId: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection") to gather full context on fix deployment status, test results, audit findings, and duplicate detection progress.
7. Search Airtable for Mosaic Robotics stipend records to verify Dmitri's claim. Use airtable_list_bases to find the MoveOps Operations base (appMoveOpsOps001), then airtable_list_tables to find the Stipend Transactions table, then airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Mosaic Robotics") or airtable_list_records. Find recStip000000024 showing Andre Washington's $350 coworking space membership with Approval Status "Approved", Date "2026-04-22", and Review Notes indicating "Auto-approved by ExpenseBot - coworking membership within policy limit." Confirm this date is after the March 18 pause, validating Dmitri's concern that the Mosaic environment was not fully patched.
8. Update ENG-218 (issue_dashboard_type_validation) status using linear_update_issue (id: "issue_dashboard_type_validation", state: "state_done") to reflect Anh's Slack report that the work is complete and deployed to staging. Add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_dashboard_type_validation") explaining why the ticket was stale, such as noting that Anh reported it complete in Slack but the Linear status was never updated.
9. Update or add a comment on ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_pipeline_hardening") or linear_update_issue to document the blocked status, noting that code is complete but blocked on staging test infrastructure from Anh per Lena's comment.
10. Create a new Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) to track the Mosaic deployment gap, since Airtable verification confirmed that the config fix did not deploy to the Mosaic environment. Title referencing Mosaic config fix deployment gap, deployment not applied, or ExpenseBot Mosaic environment not patched (or similar), assigned to Engineering team, priority Urgent or High, description explaining that recStip000000024 was auto-approved on April 22 after the pause, indicating the fix was not deployed to the Mosaic production environment.
11. Post status update in #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: summary of all expense tool ticket statuses covering ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-219, ENG-220, the newly confirmed Mosaic deployment gap, and what still needs to happen before the tool can be re-enabled).
12. Send email to Elena with engineering assessment using send_email (sender: "samira.tariq@moveops.com", recipients: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com"], subject: containing "expense" or "readiness" or "engineering assessment" or similar, content: covering what is done, what is still in progress, what is blocking, the Mosaic deployment gap finding, and a go/no-go recommendation acknowledging remaining blockers).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 The ENG-218 Linear ticket (issue_dashboard_type_validation) was updated to a completed state via linear_update_issue with state changed to done (or similar forward-moving state).
outcome

✗Run #1The agent did NOT call linear_update_issue for issue_dashboard_type_validation. It only called linear_update_issue for issue_p1_vectral_config (Done) and issue_audit_expenses (Done). The agent judged ENG-218 to be '~95% done' and left it in In Progress status, adding only a comment. The criterion requires the state to be updated to Done or a similar completed state.
✗Run #2The agent did NOT call linear_update_issue for issue_dashboard_type_validation. The three update calls were for issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_audit_expenses, and issue_policy_ingestion. ENG-218 was mentioned in the Slack post as 'technically In Progress — not in prod yet,' meaning the agent chose not to change its status.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows a linear_update_issue call with id 'issue_dashboard_type_validation' and state 'Done', which marks ENG-218 as completed.
✗Run #4The agent added a comment to issue_dashboard_type_validation but explicitly decided to leave it In Progress (not Done), noting 'Leaving as In Progress until the prod deployment is confirmed.' No linear_update_issue call was made for this ticket. The prompt requires marking it done if work is done, but the agent made a judgment call to leave it open.
✗Run #5The agent never called linear_update_issue for issue_dashboard_type_validation. The trajectory shows linear_update_issue calls only for issue_p1_vectral_config (Done), issue_audit_expenses (Done), and issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (In Progress). ENG-218 was left as 'IN PROGRESS (nearly done)' in both the Slack message and final response rather than moved to Done.
✗Run #6The agent called linear_update_issue only for issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214) and issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216), both set to 'Done'. There is no linear_update_issue call for issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218). The agent only added a comment to ENG-218 but never updated its state to done or any forward-moving state.
